solution:

⇒Ix=tanx.sinx-2022+∫2022sinx-2022dx-2022∫sinx-2022dx

Now at  x=π4

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given,

Ix=∫x+1x1+xex2dx

Now let 1+xex=t

⇒exx+1dx=dt

So, Ix=∫1t-1t2dt

⇒Ix=∫1-t2+t2t-1t2dt

⇒Ix=∫-t+1t2+1t-1dt

⇒Ix=∫-1t-1t2+1t-1dt

⇒Ix=-lnt+1t+lnt-1+C

⇒Ix=lnxexxex+1+1xex+1+C

Also given,limx→∞Ix=0

⇒limx→∞Ix=limx→∞ln1-1xex+1+1xex+1+C

⇒limx→∞Ix=ln1-0+0+C

⇒C=0

Now finding,

I1=lnee+1+1e+1=1+1e+1-ln(e+1)

⇒I1=e+2e+1-lne+1

subject: Mathematics
solution:
$\int \sin^{-11/2}x\cos^{-5/2}x\,dx$.
Since $m+n = -8$ (negative even), substitute $t = \cot x$, $dx = \frac{-dt}{1+t^2}$.
Integral becomes $-\int \frac{(1+t^2)^3}{t^{5/2}}dt = -\int(t^{-5/2}+3t^{-1/2}+3t^{3/2}+t^{7/2})dt$.
$= \frac{2}{3}t^{-3/2} - 6t^{1/2} - \frac{6}{5}t^{5/2} - \frac{2}{9}t^{9/2} + C$.
Comparing: $\frac{p_1}{q_1} = \frac{2}{9}$, $\frac{p_2}{q_2} = \frac{6}{5}$, $\frac{p_3}{q_3} = \frac{6}{1}$, $\frac{p_4}{q_4} = \frac{2}{3}$.
$\frac{15p_1p_2p_3p_4}{q_1q_2q_3q_4} = \frac{15 \cdot 2 \cdot 6 \cdot 6 \cdot 2}{9 \cdot 5 \cdot 1 \cdot 3} = \frac{2160}{135} = 16$.

subject: Mathematics
solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \int x^3 \sin x d x=-x^3 \cos x+\int 3 x^2 \cos x d x \\
& =-x^3 \cos x+3 x^2 \sin x-\int 6 x \sin x d x \\
& =-x^3 \cos x+3 x^2 \sin x+6 x \cos x-6 \sin x+c
\end{aligned}$

So $g(x)=-x^3 \cos x+3 x^2 \sin x+6 x \cos x-6 \sin x$
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{g}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)=\frac{3 \pi^2}{4}-6 \\
& \mathrm{~g}^{\prime}(\mathrm{x})=-3 \mathrm{x}^2 \cos \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{x}^3 \sin \mathrm{x}+6 \cos \mathrm{x}-6 \cos \mathrm{x} \\
& \mathrm{~g}^{\prime}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)=\frac{\pi^3}{8} \\
& 8\left(\mathrm{~g}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)+\mathrm{g}^{\prime}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)\right)=\pi^3+6 \pi^2-48
\end{aligned}$

So $\alpha+\beta-\gamma=55$

⇒fx=tanx+secx+c, c∈R
Hence, possible choice is fx=secx+tanx+12.

subject: Mathematics
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \int \frac{1}{\sqrt[5]{(x-1)^4(x+3)^6}} d x=A\left(\frac{\alpha x-1}{\beta x+3}\right)^B+C \\ & I=\int \frac{1}{(x-1)^{4 / 5}(x+3)^{6 / 5}} d x \\ & I=\int \frac{1}{\left(\frac{x-1}{x+3}\right)^{4 / 5}(x+3)^2} d x \\ & \left(\frac{x-1}{x+3}\right)=t \Rightarrow \frac{4}{(x+3)^2} d x=d t \quad t^{-4 / 5+1} \\ & I=\frac{1}{4} \int \frac{1}{t^{4 / 5}} d t=\frac{1}{4} \frac{t^{1 / 5}}{1 / 5}+c \\ & I=\frac{5}{4}\left(\frac{x-1}{x+3}\right)^{1 / 5}+C \\ & A=\frac{5}{4} \quad \alpha=\beta=1 \quad B=\frac{1}{5} \\ & \alpha+\beta+20 A B=2+20 \times \frac{5}{4} \times \frac{1}{5}=7\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
solution:
$I(x)=\int \frac{6 d x}{\sin ^2 x(1-\cot x)^2}=\int \frac{6 \operatorname{cosec}^2 x d x}{(1-\cot x)^2}$
Put $1-\cot x=t$ $\operatorname{cosec}^2 \mathrm{xdx}=\mathrm{dt}$ $\begin{aligned} & I=\int \frac{6 d t}{t^2}=\frac{-6}{t}+c \\ & I(x)=\frac{-6}{1-\cot x} c, c=3 \\ & I(x)=3-\frac{6}{1-\cot x}, I\left(\frac{\pi}{12}\right)=3-\frac{6}{1-(2+\sqrt{3})} \\ & I\left(\frac{\pi}{12}\right)=3+\frac{6}{\sqrt{3}+1}=3+\frac{6(\sqrt{3}-1)}{2}=3 \sqrt{3}\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \text { Let } f(x)=\int\left(\frac{x^2+\sin ^2 x}{1+x^2}\right) \sec ^2 x d x \\
& =\int \frac{x^2 \sec ^2 x+\frac{\sin ^2 x}{\cos ^2 x}}{1+x^2} d x
\end{aligned}
$$

$$
\begin{aligned}
& =\int \frac{x^2 \sec ^2 x+\tan ^2 x}{1+x^2} d x \\
& =\int \frac{x^2\left(1+\tan ^2 x\right)+\tan ^2 x}{1+x^2} d x \\
& =\int \frac{x^2+\tan ^2 x\left(1+x^2\right)}{1+x^2} d x \\
& =\int \frac{x^2}{1+x^2} d x+\int \tan ^2 x d x \\
& =\int \frac{x^2+1-1}{1+x^2} \mathrm{dx}+\int\left(\sec ^2 \mathrm{x}-1 \mathrm{dx}\right. \\
& =\int 1 d x-\int \frac{d x}{1+x^2}+\int \sec ^2 x d x-\int d x \\
& =-\tan -1 x+\tan ^{-1} x+\mathrm{c} \\
& \text { Given: } f(0)=0 \\
& \Rightarrow f(0)=-\tan ^{-1} 0+\tan ^2+c \\
& \Rightarrow c=0 \\
& \therefore f(x)=-\tan ^{-1} x+\tan ^2 x
\end{aligned}
$$
Now,
$$
f(1)=-\tan ^{-1}(1)+\tan 1=\tan 1-\frac{\pi}{4}
$$

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Write $2 - x^2 = (1-x)(1+x) + 1$, so the integrand splits as $e^x\left(\sqrt{\frac{1+x}{1-x}} + \frac{1}{\sqrt{1+x}(1-x)^{3/2}}\right)$.
Let $g(x) = \sqrt{\frac{1+x}{1-x}}$. Then $g'(x) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{1+x}(1-x)^{3/2}}$.
Using $\int e^x(g + g')dx = e^x g + C$: $f(x) = e^x\sqrt{\frac{1+x}{1-x}} + C$.
$f(0) = 1 + C = 0 \Rightarrow C = -1$.
$f(1/2) = e^{1/2}\sqrt{\frac{3/2}{1/2}} - 1 = \sqrt{e}\cdot\sqrt{3} - 1 = \sqrt{3e} - 1$.

$\int \frac{5 \tan x}{\tan x-2} d x=\int \frac{5 \sin x}{\sin x-2 \cos x} d x$
$\Rightarrow \int\left[\frac{2(\cos x+2 \sin x)+(\sin x-2 \cos x)}{\sin x-2 \cos x}\right] d x$
$=2 \int\left(\frac{\cos x+2 \sin x}{\sin x-2 \cos x}\right) d x+\int d x+k$
$=2 \log |\sin x-2 \cos x|+x+k \quad \therefore a=2$

solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \sqrt{2} \int \frac{\sin x d x}{\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{4}\right)}=\sqrt{2} \int \frac{\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{\pi}{4}\right) d x}{\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{4}\right)} \\
& =\sqrt{2} \int\left(\cos \frac{\pi}{4}+\cot \left(x-\frac{\pi}{4}\right) \sin \frac{\pi}{4}\right) d x \\
& =\int d x+\int \cot \left(x-\frac{\pi}{4}\right) d x \\
& =x+\ln \left|\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{4}\right)\right|+c .
\end{aligned}
$$

⇒I=∫sec2xtanxcosθ-sinθdx+∫cosec2xcosθ-cotxsinθdx

So, I=I1+I2 ....let

For I1, let tanxcosθ-sinθ=t2

⇒cosθ×sec2xdx=2tdt

⇒sec2xdx=2tdtcosθ

For I2, let cosθ-cotxsinθ=z2

⇒cosec2xdx=2zdzsinθ

⇒I=∫2tdtcosθ×t+∫2zdzsinθ×z

⇒I=2secθtanxcosθ-sinθ+2cosecθcosθ-cotxsinθ

⇒A×B=22×4sinθcosθ

⇒A×B=8cosec2θ

solution: $\int \frac{d x}{\cos x-\sin x}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \int \frac{1}{\cos \left(x+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)} d x=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \int \sec \left(x+\frac{\pi}{4}\right) d x=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \log \left|\tan \left(\frac{x}{2}+\frac{3 \pi}{8}\right)\right|+C$
